mission.title: Install and Configure Bank Vaults on Kubernetes
mission.description: Bank-Vaults is a CLI tool designed to simplify the initialization, unsealing, and configuration of Hashicorp Vault, making it easier to manage secrets in cloud-native environments. Installing Bank-Vaults allows you to streamline secret management and enhance security in your Kubernetes applications.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for Bank-Vaults to run in. This helps to isolate the resources used by Bank-Vaults from other applications in your cluster.

steps.title: Deploy Bank-Vaults
steps.description: Apply the necessary manifests to deploy Bank-Vaults. You can find the manifests in the Bank-Vaults GitHub repository.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check if the Bank-Vaults pods are running successfully.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Bank-Vaults will show the Bank-Vaults pods in a running state when you execute the 'kubectl get pods' command. Additionally, resource limits and RBAC settings should be applied correctly to ensure optimal operation.

uninstall.title: Remove Bank-Vaults
uninstall.description: To uninstall Bank-Vaults, delete the resources created in the bank-vaults namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Bank-Vaults
upgrade.description: To upgrade Bank-Vaults, apply the new version of the manifests. Make sure to check for the latest version on the GitHub releases page.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: If the Bank-Vaults pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors using the command: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n bank-vaults`.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter issues related to resource limits, ensure that your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated for the Bank-Vaults deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Network Issues
troubleshooting.description: If you cannot access the Bank-Vaults service, verify the service configuration and ensure that the correct ports are exposed.

prerequisites.description: Ensure that you have kubectl installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.
